Homework 2 CVX-OPT 10-725

1 Subgradients and Proximal Operators

(i)

Let g1, g2 ∈ ∂f(x) , therefore we have: (1)+(2): f(y) ≥ f(x) + [tg1 + (1 − t)g2]⊤(y − x) Thus, g = tg1 + (1 − t)g2 ∈ ∂f and the subdifferential ∂f(x) is a convex set.

(ii)

Recall that:

The normal cone of a set C at a boundary point x0 is the set of all vectors y such that y⊤(x − x0) ≤ 0 for all x ∈ C (i.e., the set of vectors that define a supporting hyperplane to C at x0)

Prove: ∂f(x) ∈ NC(x),  where C = {y : f(y) ≤ f(x)} Let y0 be the boundary point, thus and C = {y : f(y) ≤ f(x0)},

We need to prove: ∂f(x0)⊤(y − y0) ≤ 0 for all y ∈ C.

We know that ∂f(x0) = {g ∈ ℝn : f(y) ≥ f(x0) + g⊤(y − x0)}, thus: And f(y) − f(y0) ≤ 0 , so 0 ≥ f(y) − f(y0) ≥ g⊤(y − y0) . Thus, ∂f(x0)⊤(y − y0) ≤ 0

(iii)

Let . (iv)

We need to prove: ||y||p ≥ ||x||p + g⊤(y − x),  where g ∈ ∂f(x) Let z* = argmax||z||q ≤ 1z⊤x, therefore, z*⊤x = ||x||p

Thus: ||x||p + z*⊤(y − x) = ||x||p + z*⊤y − z*⊤x = z*⊤y ≤ ||y||p Therefore, z* ∈ ∂f(x)

(i) Note that A ∈ 𝕊+n , and therefore det(I + tA) > 0 (I + tA) is invertible.

(ii)

Lambert W function: the inverse of the fuction f(x) = xex; That is, the W function satisfies: W(x)eW(x) = x

The graph of y = W(x) for real x < 6 and y > −4. The upper branch (blue) with y ≥ −1 is the graph of the function W0 (principal branch), the lower branch (magenta) with y ≤ −1 is the graph of the function W−1. The minimum value of x is at {−1/e, −1}
